sections.summary: Heading only; group standard title for unripened cheese.
Heading noting that the standard includes fresh cheese under CXS 221-2001.
Provides the adoption and amendment history: adopted in 2001 and amended in 2008, 2010, 2013, 2018, 2021 and 2022.

Notes a 2022 amendment that replaced prior Section 7.3 labelling text for non‑retail containers with the instruction to follow the General Standard for the Labelling of Non‑Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).

Applies to unripened (including fresh) cheeses intended for direct consumption or further processing and states that more specific Codex standards for individual varieties prevail over this group standard where applicable.
Defines unripened and fresh cheeses as cheeses conforming to the General Standard for Cheese (CXS 283-1978) that are ready for consumption shortly after manufacture.
Heading introducing essential composition and quality factors, with details on raw materials and permitted ingredients provided in subsections.
Specifies raw materials as milk and/or products obtained from milk.

Lists permitted ingredients for unripened cheese: starter cultures and other harmless micro‑organisms, rennet or other safe coagulating enzymes, sodium chloride, potable water, vinegar, gelatine and starches (as stabilizers only in functionally necessary amounts per Good Manufacturing Practice), and rice/corn/potato flours and starches as surface anti‑caking agents only in functionally necessary amounts per Good Manufacturing Practice.

Specifies which additive functional classes are technologically justified for unripened/fresh cheese (food category 01.6.1) referring to Tables 1–3 of the GSFA (CXS 192-1995), with a table indicating permitted classes for cheese mass and surface/rind and footnotes restricting certain uses (e.g., surface treatment of sliced/cut/shredded/grated cheese, whipped products, edible rinds) and limiting stabilizer/thickener use to what is functionally necessary as per Section 3.2.
States that labelling of non-retail containers should follow the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).
Requires that methods of analysis and sampling for checking compliance use the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to this standard's provisions.
